info.name: Domestic RTP Payment Service API
info.description: Postman collection for ISO-native CreateDomesticPayment and GetDomesticPaymentStatus API flows. Uses pain.001.001.09 requests and pain.002.001.10 responses. The HK clearing and settlement behavior is simulator-only. pacs.008 is internal only. Allowed X-Mock-Scenario values: success, rejection, suspicious_proxy_or_account, pending, timeout, internal_failure. Classic Payment Rail Simulation journey adds RTP baseline, ACH Direct Credit JSON batch, RTGS corporate/FI JSON payment, and FI correspondent comparison scenarios.

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Document xmlns="urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09">
  <CstmrCdtTrfInitn>
    <GrpHdr><MsgId>MSG-20260524-0001</MsgId><CreDtTm>2026-05-24T10:00:00+08:00</CreDtTm><NbOfTxs>1</NbOfTxs></GrpHdr>
    <PmtInf>
      <PmtInfId>PMT-20260524-SUCCESS</PmtInfId><PmtMtd>TRF</PmtMtd>
      <Dbtr><Nm>Acme Treasury HK</Nm></Dbtr>
      <DbtrAcct><Id><Othr><Id>000111222333</Id></Othr></Id></DbtrAcct>
      <DbtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>CIBBHKHH</BICFI></FinInstnId></DbtrAgt>
      <CdtTrfTxInf>
        <PmtId><InstrId>INSTR-20260524-0001</InstrId><EndToEndId>INV-2026-0001</EndToEndId></PmtId>
        <Amt><InstdAmt Ccy="HKD">1250.50</InstdAmt></Amt>
        <CdtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>SUPPHKHH</BICFI></FinInstnId></CdtrAgt>
        <Cdtr><Nm>Supplier HK Limited</Nm></Cdtr>
        <CdtrAcct><Id><Othr><Id>000123456789</Id></Othr></Id></CdtrAcct>
        <RmtInf><Ustrd>Invoice INV-2026-0001 supplier settlement</Ustrd><Strd><CdtrRefInf><Ref>SCREF-2026-0001</Ref></CdtrRefInf></Strd></RmtInf>
      </CdtTrfTxInf>
    </PmtInf>
  </CstmrCdtTrfInitn>
</Document>

throw new Error('Set readOnlyJwtToken to a JWT generated with only payments:read before running this request.');

throw new Error('readOnlyJwtToken must include payments:read.');
pm.test('authorization failure returns forbidden', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(403); });

request.description: Run this request first to create the original payment for idempotency conflict testing. Then run Create Payment - Idempotency Conflict.
Run Create Payment - Idempotency Conflict Setup first. This request then reuses the same conflictIdempotencyKey with different normalized payment semantics to assert 409 Conflict.

body.raw: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><Document xmlns="urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09"><CstmrCdtTrfInitn><GrpHdr><MsgId>MSG-20260524-CONFLICT-DIFFERENT</MsgId><CreDtTm>2026-05-24T10:00:00+08:00</CreDtTm><NbOfTxs>1</NbOfTxs></GrpHdr><PmtInf><PmtInfId>PMT-20260524-CONFLICT-DIFFERENT</PmtInfId><PmtMtd>TRF</PmtMtd><Dbtr><Nm>Acme Treasury HK</Nm></Dbtr><DbtrAcct><Id><Othr><Id>000111222333</Id></Othr></Id></DbtrAcct><DbtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>CIBBHKHH</BICFI></FinInstnId></DbtrAgt><CdtTrfTxInf><PmtId><InstrId>INSTR-20260524-CONFLICT-DIFFERENT</InstrId><EndToEndId>INV-2026-CONFLICT-DIFFERENT</EndToEndId></PmtId><Amt><InstdAmt Ccy="HKD">52.00</InstdAmt></Amt><CdtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>SUPPHKHH</BICFI></FinInstnId></CdtrAgt><Cdtr><Nm>Supplier HK Limited</Nm></Cdtr><CdtrAcct><Id><Othr><Id>000123456789</Id></Othr></Id></CdtrAcct><RmtInf><Ustrd>Idempotency conflict different semantics</Ustrd></RmtInf></CdtTrfTxInf></PmtInf></CstmrCdtTrfInitn></Document>

response.name: 409 Conflict - Idempotency
response.body: {"code":"IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT","message":"Idempotency key was reused with a different request body","status":409,"correlationId":"postman-local-correlation-001"}

item.name: Classic Payment Rail Simulation
item.description: Manual product and architecture journey comparing RTP, ACH Direct Credit, RTGS, and FI correspondent arrangements. RTP is immediate domestic payment API baseline; ACH is JSON batch clearing with settlement/status summaries; RTGS is gross settlement with finality/liquidity outcomes; FI correspondent is a separate nostro/vostro/loro arrangement that can be compared with but is not necessarily RTP/ACH/RTGS. Token scopes used in this journey: payments:create, payments:read, ach-batches:create, ach-batches:read, rtgs-payments:create, rtgs-payments:read.

request.description: RTP baseline using the existing domestic real-time payment flow. Expected HTTP status: 200; expected lifecycle status: ACSC. Common setup: jwtToken must include payments:create and payments:read.

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Document xmlns="urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09"><CstmrCdtTrfInitn><GrpHdr><MsgId>MSG-CLASSIC-RTP-0001</MsgId><CreDtTm>2026-06-01T10:00:00+08:00</CreDtTm><NbOfTxs>1</NbOfTxs></GrpHdr><PmtInf><PmtInfId>PMT-CLASSIC-RTP</PmtInfId><PmtMtd>TRF</PmtMtd><Dbtr><Nm>Acme Treasury HK</Nm></Dbtr><DbtrAcct><Id><Othr><Id>000111222333</Id></Othr></Id></DbtrAcct><DbtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>CIBBHKHH</BICFI></FinInstnId></DbtrAgt><CdtTrfTxInf><PmtId><InstrId>INSTR-CLASSIC-RTP-0001</InstrId><EndToEndId>INV-CLASSIC-RTP-0001</EndToEndId></PmtId><Amt><InstdAmt Ccy="HKD">1250.50</InstdAmt></Amt><CdtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>SUPPHKHH</BICFI></FinInstnId></CdtrAgt><Cdtr><Nm>Supplier HK Limited</Nm></Cdtr><CdtrAcct><Id><Othr><Id>000123456789</Id></Othr></Id></CdtrAcct><RmtInf><Ustrd>Classic rail RTP baseline</Ustrd></RmtInf></CdtTrfTxInf></PmtInf></CstmrCdtTrfInitn></Document>

exec: pm.test('RTP baseline returns pain.002 ACSC', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: RTP baseline status lookup. Expected HTTP status: 200; expected lifecycle status: ACSC. Common setup: run RTP Baseline - Create Payment first; no Idempotency-Key is used for status.

exec: pm.test('RTP status returns pain.002', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: ACH Direct Credit JSON batch settled scenario. Expected HTTP status: 202; expected lifecycle status: SETTLED. Key fields: batchId, rail ACH, entryCount, totalAmount, entry-level status summary. Common setup: use a fresh idempotency key and a JWT with the create scope; X-Mock-Scenario aligns with deterministic simulator mappings.

exec: pm.test('ACH settled batch accepted', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(202); });

item.name: ACH Direct Credit - Get Batch Status

request.description: ACH Direct Credit status lookup. Expected HTTP status: 200; expected lifecycle status: latest resource status. Common setup: run a create request first to capture the ID; no Idempotency-Key is used for status.

exec: pm.test('ACH status returns JSON batch summary', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: ACH Direct Credit JSON batch partial return scenario. Expected HTTP status: 202; expected lifecycle status: PARTIALLY_RETURNED. Key fields: batch-level reason, returned entry, settled remaining entries, entry-level status summary. Common setup: use a fresh idempotency key and a JWT with the create scope; X-Mock-Scenario aligns with deterministic simulator mappings.

exec: pm.test('ACH partially returned batch accepted', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(202); });

request.description: ACH Direct Credit JSON batch rejected scenario. Expected HTTP status: 202; expected lifecycle status: REJECTED. Key fields: batch-level reason, rejected entry statuses, entry-level status summary. Common setup: use a fresh idempotency key and a JWT with the create scope; X-Mock-Scenario aligns with deterministic simulator mappings.

exec: pm.test('ACH rejected batch accepted with rejected lifecycle', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(202); });

request.description: Corporate RTGS JSON payment settled scenario. Expected HTTP status: 202; expected lifecycle status: SETTLED. Key fields: paymentId, rail RTGS, clientSegment CORPORATE, settlementFinality true. Common setup: use a fresh idempotency key and a JWT with the create scope; X-Mock-Scenario aligns with deterministic simulator mappings.

exec: pm.test('Corporate RTGS settled with finality', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(202); });

request.description: FI RTGS JSON payment settled scenario. Expected HTTP status: 202; expected lifecycle status: SETTLED. Key fields: paymentId, rail RTGS, clientSegment FI, instructing/instructed BICs, settlementFinality true. Common setup: use a fresh idempotency key and a JWT with the create scope; X-Mock-Scenario aligns with deterministic simulator mappings.

exec: pm.test('FI RTGS settled with finality', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(202); });

request.description: FI RTGS queued-for-liquidity scenario. Expected HTTP status: 202; expected lifecycle status: QUEUED_FOR_LIQUIDITY. Key fields: clientSegment FI, settlementFinality false, RTGS_LIQUIDITY_QUEUE reason. Common setup: use a fresh idempotency key and a JWT with the create scope; X-Mock-Scenario aligns with deterministic simulator mappings.

exec: pm.test('FI RTGS queues for liquidity', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(202); });

request.description: RTGS rejected scenario. Expected HTTP status: 202; expected lifecycle status: REJECTED. Key fields: settlementFinality false and RTGS_PAYMENT_REJECTED reason. Common setup: use a fresh idempotency key and a JWT with the create scope; X-Mock-Scenario aligns with deterministic simulator mappings.

exec: pm.test('RTGS rejected lifecycle returned', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(202); });

request.description: RTGS status lookup. Expected HTTP status: 200; expected lifecycle status: latest resource status. Common setup: run a create request first to capture the ID; no Idempotency-Key is used for status.

exec: pm.test('RTGS status returns JSON payment state', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: Comparison note: FI correspondent payment is a separate arrangement, not necessarily RTP/ACH/RTGS. Use existing FI correspondent requests to demonstrate correspondent account path context: nostro, vostro, loro, correspondentOrIntermediaryBic, and masked simulated account reference. Expected HTTP status: 200 when querying an existing FI payment status. Common setup: run Create FI Payment - Accepted first to capture fiPaymentId.

exec: pm.test('FI correspondent comparison returns status context', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

item.name: Payment Rail Recommendation Copilot
item.description: Deterministic simulator recommendation journey for RTP, ACH, RTGS, and FI correspondent arrangements. Requires payment-rail-recommendations:create. Recommendation requests do not use Idempotency-Key and do not create payment resources. Responses include intentFit tradeoff explanation and nextApiGuidance for candidate follow-up APIs.

item.name: Recommend Rail - RTP Immediate Low Value

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. Immediate low-value domestic USD intent should recommend RTP and DOMESTIC_REAL_TIME_CLEARING.

exec: pm.test('recommended rail is RTP', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('RECOMMENDED'); pm.expect(body.recommendedOption.rail).to.eql('RTP'); });
pm.test('arrangement is domestic real-time clearing', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().recommendedOption.arrangement).to.eql('DOMESTIC_REAL_TIME_CLEARING'); });

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. Multiple-payment domestic USD vendor batch should recommend ACH and BATCH_CLEARING_NET_SETTLEMENT.

exec: pm.test('recommended rail is ACH', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('RECOMMENDED'); pm.expect(body.recommendedOption.rail).to.eql('ACH'); });
pm.test('arrangement is batch clearing net settlement', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().recommendedOption.arrangement).to.eql('BATCH_CLEARING_NET_SETTLEMENT'); });

item.name: Recommend Rail - ACH Missing Max Single Amount Warning

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. Batch intent without maxSingleAmount should recommend ACH and include MAX_SINGLE_AMOUNT_NOT_PROVIDED.

exec: pm.test('recommended rail is ACH', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('RECOMMENDED'); pm.expect(body.recommendedOption.rail).to.eql('ACH'); });

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. Batch finality/high-value conflict should keep ACH primary and return BATCH_HIGH_VALUE_ENTRY_REVIEW with RTGS alternative.

exec: pm.test('recommended rail is ACH', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('RECOMMENDED'); pm.expect(body.recommendedOption.rail).to.eql('ACH'); });

pm.test('RTGS conflict guidance is present', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.warnings.map(w => w.code)).to.include('BATCH_HIGH_VALUE_ENTRY_REVIEW'); pm.expect(body.alternatives.map(a => a.rail)).to.include('RTGS'); });

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. High-value finality-required corporate intent should recommend RTGS.

exec: pm.test('recommended rail is RTGS', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('RECOMMENDED'); pm.expect(body.recommendedOption.rail).to.eql('RTGS'); });
pm.test('arrangement is domestic interbank gross settlement', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().recommendedOption.arrangement).to.eql('DOMESTIC_INTERBANK_GROSS_SETTLEMENT'); });

item.name: Recommend Rail - RTGS FI Gross Settlement

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. FI domestic gross settlement intent should recommend RTGS, not FI_CORRESPONDENT.

exec: pm.test('recommended rail is RTGS', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('RECOMMENDED'); pm.expect(body.recommendedOption.rail).to.eql('RTGS'); });

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. FI intent with CORRESPONDENT_ACCOUNT_PATH should recommend FI_CORRESPONDENT.

exec: pm.test('recommended rail is FI_CORRESPONDENT', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('RECOMMENDED'); pm.expect(body.recommendedOption.rail).to.eql('FI_CORRESPONDENT'); });
pm.test('arrangement is correspondent account path', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().recommendedOption.arrangement).to.eql('CORRESPONDENT_ACCOUNT_PATH'); });

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. Valid cross-border intent should return UNSUPPORTED and CROSS_BORDER_NOT_SUPPORTED.

exec: pm.test('cross-border unsupported', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('UNSUPPORTED'); pm.expect(body.warnings.map(w => w.code)).to.include('CROSS_BORDER_NOT_SUPPORTED'); });

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. Valid non-USD intent should return UNSUPPORTED and NON_USD_NOT_SUPPORTED.

exec: pm.test('non-USD unsupported', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.recommendationStatus).to.eql('UNSUPPORTED'); pm.expect(body.warnings.map(w => w.code)).to.include('NON_USD_NOT_SUPPORTED'); });

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 403. Token without payment-rail-recommendations:create should return FORBIDDEN.

Create a simulated mandate before collection. Expected HTTP status: 202; expected status: ACTIVE. Common setup: use a JWT with mandates:create and a fresh Idempotency-Key. Default scenario variable value is mandate_active.
Get simulated mandate status. Expected HTTP status: 200. Status query does not require Idempotency-Key.
Cancel an active or pending simulated mandate. Expected HTTP status: 200; expected status: CANCELLED. Use a fresh mandate for this request if you already used it for collection demos.

item.name: Collection - US ACH Direct Debit Collected

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 202. Creates a US ACH Direct Debit batch-style collection using an existing mandateReference. Common setup: collectionJwtToken must include collections:create.

exec: pm.test('collection is collected', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.collectionProfile).to.eql('US_ACH_DIRECT_DEBIT_BATCH'); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql('COLLECTED'); pm.environment.set('collectionId', body.collectionId); });

item.name: Collection - US ACH Direct Debit Replay

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 202. Run after Collection - US ACH Direct Debit Collected; same Idempotency-Key and same body should replay the original response.

exec: pm.test('idempotent replay returns original collection', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().collectionId).to.eql(pm.environment.get('collectionId')); });

item.name: Collection - US ACH Direct Debit Idempotency Conflict

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 409. Run after Collection - US ACH Direct Debit Collected; same Idempotency-Key with changed semantics should return IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT.

exec: pm.test('idempotency conflict returns conflict code', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().code).to.eql('IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT'); });

item.name: Collection - HK FPS Direct Debit Completed

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 202. Creates an HK FPS/eDDA-style direct debit collection using an existing mandateReference. This is simulator-only and does not set up eDDA.

exec: pm.test('HK FPS collection completed', function () { const body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.collectionProfile).to.eql('HK_FPS_DIRECT_DEBIT'); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql('COMPLETED'); pm.environment.set('collectionId', body.collectionId); });

request.description: Expected HTTP status: 200. Status query does not require Idempotency-Key. Common setup: run a collection create request first or set collectionId manually.
